data.title: FRONTIER AIRLINES ANNOUNCES DISCOUNTED RESCUE FARES TO SUPPORT SPIRIT AIRLINES CUSTOMERS
data.description: Frontier Airlines has disclosed that they are now servicing over 100 routes that were previously operated by Spirit Airlines. This announcement came on May 2, 2026. In response to Spirit Airlines' discontinuation of services, Frontier Airlines has introduced rescue fare discounts across their entire network. Additionally, Frontier Airlines is launching a special deal called the "GoWild All-You-Can-Fly Summer Pass" priced at $199. This pass is aimed at assisting travelers impacted by the closure of Spirit Airlines.

data.title: Analyst says no need for new narrative for Bitcoin to reach $100K. Attention on tech sectors may hinder crypto growth.
data.description: According to a crypto analyst, the cryptocurrency market may find it challenging to establish a compelling narrative that drives prices up, as attention is increasingly shifting towards various other technology industries.

data.title: Low-fare flight innovator, Spirit Airlines, closes down after second bankruptcy in two years amidst years of strife.
data.description: Spirit Airlines, known for its low fares, is facing its second bankruptcy in two years following a period of financial difficulties.

data.title: Spirit Airlines Begins Orderly Wind-Down of Operations
data.description: DANIA BEACH, Fla., May 2, 2026 - Spirit Aviation Holdings, Inc., the parent company of Spirit Airlines, LLC, has made the unfortunate decision to begin the systematic shutdown of operations. This has led to the cancellation of all flights. The company's announcement signifies a significant impact on air travel, affecting a considerable number of passengers.

data.title: Bitcoin continues to rally as options suggest 25% chance of reaching $84K in May, thanks to institutional investors.
data.description: The main factors contributing to the increase in the price of Bitcoin (BTC) are institutional investors and companies acquiring the cryptocurrency in large quantities. These entities continue to accumulate Bitcoin even without a significant increase in bullish leverage.

data.title: Nvidia's China Exposure Back In Focus As Senator Coons Probes H200 Export Claims
data.description: Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) is seeking clarification from the Commerce Department following conflicting statements by Secretary Howard Lutnick and Nvidia Corp. CEO Jensen Huang regarding the sale of advanced U.S. AI chips to China. Lutnick stated during an April 22 Senate Appropriations subcommittee hearing that the U.S. had not authorized Nvidia's H200 AI chips to be sold to Chinese firms, contradicting Huang's earlier comments that approvals for the chip sales had been obtained from both U.S. and Chinese authorities. Coons raised concerns in a letter, pointing out the discrepancies in the statements and demanding clarity on the situation. The full story is available on a financial news website.

data.title: Final rules for stablecoin yield in CLARITY Act are set, prompting Galaxy Digital to anticipate more opposition from banking sector.
data.description: According to Alex Thorn, head of research at Galaxy Digital, the banking sector is anticipated to heighten its resistance endeavors in response to the finalized stablecoin yield provisions. Thorn foresees a rise in opposition from the banking industry post the release of these provisions.
Mag 7 Just Committed $710 Billion To AI Capex

data.title: Japanese sento, vital to elderly, face crisis due to high fuel costs amid Iran conflict shocks.
data.description: Rising fuel costs pose a threat to Japanese sento, traditional bathhouses that have been declining over the years but continue to serve as crucial social hubs for the country's elderly population.

data.title: Metropolitan Issues Statement on Lower Basin Plan for Near-Term Colorado River Operations
data.description: In Los Angeles, Shivaji Deshmukh, the General Manager of Metropolitan Water District, has released a statement on the updated Lower Basin proposal for Colorado River operations until 2028, sent to the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation. Over the past year, Metropolitan has been striving to reach an agreement among all Colorado River water users for operations after 2026, aiming to enhance sustainability and reliability on the river. Regrettably, all seven states have contributed to this effort, aiming to ensure the river's well-being.

data.title: Forefront Tech Holdings Acquisition Corp Announces Closing of $100,000,000 Initial Public Offering
data.description: Today, May 02, 2026, Forefront Tech Holdings Acquisition Corp completed its initial public offering (IPO) on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol FTHAU. The Company closed the IPO of 10,000,000 units at $10.00 per unit, generating gross proceeds of $100 million before underwriting discounts and offering expenses deduction. The units commenced trading on The Nasdaq Global Market tier on April 30, 2026.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures: Stock Market Hits High After Bullish Pause; Apple, Broadcom In Buy Zones
data.description: After a brief pause, the stock market saw a late-week rebound with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq reaching new highs. Apple and Broadcom are currently in favorable buy zones. Dow Jones Futures reflect a positive trend as the market hits a high following the bullish pause.

data.title: Always A Runner Claims the Lilies for the 152nd Running of the Longines Kentucky Oaks
data.description: For the first time ever, the Kentucky Oaks will be broadcast in primetime on NBC. This is a significant milestone for the event, as it allows for a larger audience to tune in and witness the prestigious horse race. The move to primetime signifies the growing popularity and importance of the Kentucky Oaks, which showcases three-year-old thoroughbred fillies. This change in broadcasting time is expected to attract a record number of viewers, with an estimated audience of millions anticipated to watch the race on NBC. This decision highlights the increasing appeal and appeal of horse racing events to a mainstream audience.
Elizabeth Warren Criticizes Trump Family's Reported Crypto Ties With Entity Linked To Sanctioned Individuals (UPDATED)

data.title: Larry Kellerman, Fermi's Chief Power Officer and Architect of Its 17 GW Energy Infrastructure, Accepts Board Nomination
data.description: In a recent announcement on May 1, 2026, Toby Neugebauer, the co-founder and primary shareholder of Fermi America (trading as FRMI on both NASDAQ and LSE), revealed his nomination of Larry Kellerman to become a new member of the Fermi Board of Directors. Kellerman holds the role of Chief Power Officer at Fermi America and his potential appointment is seen as a strategic move within the company.

data.title: GoldHaven Announces Additional $700,000 Critical Mineral Flow-Through Financing
data.description: GoldHaven Resources Corp., based in Vancouver, Canada, has revealed plans for an upcoming non-brokered flow-through private placement financing round. The company aims to raise up to $700,000 in gross proceeds through this offering. This will involve issuing a maximum of 2,641,509 flow-through shares at a price set at $0.265 per share.

data.title: Stablecoin yield rules set; Galaxy Digital predicts banking sector opposition to rise after release of final provisions.
data.description: Alex Thorn, who is the head of research at Galaxy Digital, anticipates that the banking sector will escalate their resistance actions subsequent to the unveiling of the last stablecoin yield regulations. Thorn foresees a rise in opposition efforts from banks as a response to the final provisions regarding stablecoin yields.

data.title: Think Together Hosts Third Annual "Think Together, Build Together" Volunteer Event to Support Summer Learning for Students Across California
data.description: A recent event in Santa Ana, California saw Think Together, a division of Think Education, putting together 2,000 classroom kits for summer learning experiences. The third annual Think Together, Build Together kit build event gathered donors and contributors to make this initiative possible.

data.title: San Diego County Credit Union Announces San Diego Superior Court Has Denied California Coast Credit Union's Motion for Preliminary Injunction to Compel Merger
data.description: On May 1, 2026, San Diego County Credit Union (SDCCU), a major locally-owned financial institution in Southern California, released a statement in response to the San Diego Superior Court's ruling denying California Coast Credit Union's appeal. SDCCU emphasized their commitment to providing reliable financial services to the community. This development follows a legal dispute involving significant assets and market share between the two credit unions in the region. The decision by the court underscores the competitive landscape and regulatory framework within the financial sector, impacting thousands of members associated with both institutions.

data.title: Asian American Engineer of the Year Award and Conference Announces First Phase of 2025-2026 Awardees
data.description: In Santa Clara, California, on May 1, 2026, the AAEOY Executive Committee disclosed the initial phase awardees for the AAEOY 2025-2026. This includes the Distinguished Lifetime Achievement Award presented to Mr. Lip-Bu Tan, the CEO of Intel Corporation.
